result.finding: The application stores PII (Personally Identifiable Information). The owners table contains first_name, last_name, address, city, and telephone — all PII fields. Vet names are also stored. No payment data (PCI), health records (PHI), or passwords are stored. No formal data classification policy is documented in the codebase.
evidence: schema.sql (mysql): owners table contains first_name, last_name, address, city, telephone (PII fields)
schema.sql (mysql): vets table contains first_name, last_name (PII fields)
schema.sql (mysql): pets table contains name, birth_date (non-sensitive)
schema.sql (mysql): visits table contains visit_date, description (potentially sensitive pet health info)
No password, credit_card, SSN, or financial fields in schema
No @PersonalData or @Confidential annotations in source code
values: PII: Owner personal details (name, address, city, telephone)
Internal: Pet records (name, birth date, type)
No PCI, PHI, or credentials data stored
